topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the room suddenly felt thick, suffocating. I slammed my textbook shut, the sound echoing sharply in the small shared space. My eyes locked onto the open page of my journal lying innocently on the desk, the handwriting clearly mine, but annotated with roommate’s looping script. A slow, hot wave of anger washed over me, tightening my chest until it was hard to draw a full breath. I stood up abruptly, my chair scraping loudly against the floorboards. "What are you doing?" My voice came out tight, barely above a strained whisper, but it carried an edge of pure indignation. When my roommate finally looked up, a flicker of surprise crossed their face, quickly replaced by a defensive smirk. I didn't say another word; I just walked toward the desk, my jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the muscle tremor in my neck.

[story 2]

It started as a slight prickle of unease, a misplaced bookmark. Then I saw it—a page I’d been so careful to keep private, now filled with marginalia that made my blood boil. I felt a sudden, intense heat bloom in my cheeks, and I gripped the edge of my desk until my knuckles turned white. I took a deep, ragged breath, trying to keep my expression neutral, but the tremor in my hands betrayed the storm brewing inside. "Stop," I managed, the word sharp and brittle. My tone was dangerously low, vibrating with suppressed energy. I watched as my roommate slowly lowered the book, their eyes meeting mine with a challenging glint. The silence that followed was heavy, charged with unspoken accusations. I couldn't seem to look away, the urge to confront them overwhelming.

[story 3]

My stomach clenched into a hard knot. I stared at the evidence spread out on the nightstand—my private thoughts, laid bare for public consumption. A slow, simmering resentment began to bubble up, rising steadily until it felt like pressure building behind my eyes. I moved with deliberate slowness, every step measured, my gaze fixed solely on the object of my irritation. When my roommate finally acknowledged the situation, their casual demeanor seemed to grate on my nerves like sandpaper. "That is mine," I stated, the words clipped and devoid of warmth. My voice held a coldness that I hadn't intended, a stark contrast to the frantic energy thrumming beneath my skin. I crossed my arms tightly across my chest, a clear signal that this was not a discussion, but a confrontation.
